title: campanula garganica subsp. lepida
intro: Campanula garganica subsp. lepida is a subspecies of the Campanula garganica plant, commonly known as the 'Lepidophanes' or 'Garganica', which is a low-growing, spreading, and perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. Campanula garganica subsp. lepida has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Campanula garganica subsp. lepida is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
